excluded.game: Current placement and future architecture are deferred; no active landing touches Game.
excluded.worlds: Worlds remains an idea rather than an implemented applet or active refactor workload.

not_applicable.providers: Library has no selectable provider surface. Its applet descriptor intentionally publishes zero capabilities and intents; the separately tested projection owner provides its resource service.

preserve: Applet entry exercised through the focused shell profile, descriptor identity, title, UIDL, callbacks, lifecycle and clean close
Active, Archived and All views; exact search; collections; retained history; back navigation; paging and preview
Managed create/retry, rename, archive and unarchive controller actions
Managed documents, immutable captures, receipts, provenance, exact revisions, operation-key idempotency and collection semantics
Content-first inactive-bank publication, sole-head authority, exact corruption/future/orphan distinctions, cleanup and retry
Catalog/content/relationship/RID bounds continue to refuse without partial mutation until the scalable successor replaces them
Exact title/body/tag search, lifecycle/kind/media/collection filters and deterministic continuation
Disposable-index loss or damage rebuilds to authoritative identical results
Projection describe/snapshot/managed replace, immutable capture reads, exact locators, leases, stale and forged token refusal, quiescence and capacity
Inspection, sealed evidence, bounded raw export and recognized repair
Future, corrupt, ambiguous and orphan evidence remains fail-closed and export-only
Fresh-process reopen preserves managed lifecycle, captures, collections, queries, projections and the complete Gate 4 damage matrix

prerequisites.trigger: Applet repository cutover or initialization changes.
prerequisites.characterization: Start LIBRARY-APPLET-INIT-CB over an existing corpus and prove reconstruction of the same view/lens state.
prerequisites.reason: Current cold-process drivers prove the owner, not applet initialization over pre-existing state.

prerequisites.trigger: Applet repository cutover, recovery presentation or writable-state changes.
prerequisites.characterization: Show corrupt, future and recovery evidence as visibly nonempty and nonwritable at applet level.
prerequisites.reason: Fail-closed headless behavior is exact, while its UI presentation is currently inferred.

bindings: f/F in Sources -> add syndication source
p/P in Sources -> add watched page
a/A outside Sources -> set public actor
r/R outside Sources -> refresh public feed

preserve: Timeline, retained search and partial-thread navigation
Local draft create, read, edit, validate and cancel without implicit publication
Source manager creation, independent selection, enable/disable, exact confirmed removal and manual refresh
All fifteen current capability IDs, schemas, effects, target rules and handlers
Offline entry plus explicit Bluesky and configured syndication compositions
JSON Feed, RSS and Atom decoding; configured HTTP authorization, redirects, media and request bounds; explicit XIO trust boundary
Accepted attempts, success, unchanged, revision, terminal failure and recovered-indeterminate truth
Deduplication, retained last-good observations and exact stale/cancel/transport/HTTP/decode distinctions
Source, observation and draft replacement recovery; corrupt/future/blocked records do not become writable authority
Offline refusal and typed/UI manual refresh through the real Desk XIO service
Accepted, busy, success, unchanged and accepted-to-indeterminate recovery
Desk source/draft persistence across close and relaunch; ordinary Agent sees only sanitized source query/read capabilities

prerequisites.characterization: Run a spawn-isolated MP64FS journey containing a source registry, draft, successful observations and a durably accepted attempt that reopens as indeterminate.
prerequisites.reason: Current descriptor reopens and desktop relaunches occur inside one guest process.

prerequisites.characterization: Exhaust observation/blob retention and prove capacity is reported, the terminal attempt is recorded and all last-good observations/key heads remain unchanged.
prerequisites.reason: No current test reaches the production OCHK-S-CAPACITY and SREF-S-CAPACITY path.

prerequisites.characterization: Prove a valid future observation-store record is preserved and refused through both the store and owner projection.
prerequisites.reason: Source and draft future records are covered; observation-store future format is not.

prerequisites.trigger: Source-card rendering is changed by the bounded UI conversion.
prerequisites.characterization: Assert rendered accepted, succeeded, failed and recovered-indeterminate source-card states.
prerequisites.reason: Semantic projections are tested, but the exact visible state distinctions are not.

bindings: Up -> scroll one visual row older
Down -> scroll one visual row newer

preserve: Prompt/send, streaming, cancel and recovery
Locked review with approve/deny and complete audit presentation
Unicode/cell-aware wrapping, visual-row scrolling, bottom anchoring and resize reflow
Chat-only, Practice read-only and Practice assist presets with exact targets and budgets
Tool approval/denial, operand seals, audit, races, quiescence and capacity
Desk-shared Agent review, cancellation, Daybook write policy and relaunch continuity
Offline and scripted providers
OpenAI provider/source and Codex provider/source/catalog/auth paths
Masked credential cancel/submit/clear, device authorization and model/effort/tier/verbosity settings
Durable transcript and audit entries
Clear, interrupted-run normalization, corrupt-candidate fallback and recovery
Provider-neutral runtime state remains consistent through reopen and Desk relaunch

prerequisites.characterization: Distinguish a standalone-owned active run, which must confirm or cancel, from a Desk-shared lens close, which must not cancel shared work.
prerequisites.reason: L9 preserves the existing opaque callback boundary and does not change Agent's close/lifecycle ownership; current journeys still do not characterize an active-run close.

not_applicable.providers: Daybook owns a concrete Markdown document and resource owner; it has no selectable provider surface.

preserve: Calendar/agenda navigation and task, event and note capture, completion and deletion
Strict Markdown remains source of truth with exact malformed, unknown, UTF-8, entry, field and file-size distinctions
Exact save/reload, blocked-source/dirty preservation, recovery and close reload/discard negotiation
daybook.task.capture, daybook.source and daybook.agenda.markdown capability identities
Semantic Daybook RID and exact describe/snapshot/replace revisions
Two lenses, stale refusal, explicit reload/reattach, wrong-resource/read-only refusal and cold owner reactivation
Edit Source routes to Pad through the semantic resource lens
Competing commit yields stale refusal without clobber, owner survives Daybook close and relaunch
Agent gets exact Daybook facets and approved/denied task capture

prerequisites.trigger: Prompt, menu, selection or navigation controller extraction touches these actions.
prerequisites.characterization: Drive event/note prompts, delete/navigation, explicit Save/Reload and Reveal Source before changing their dispatch path.
prerequisites.reason: Model behavior and the exact UIDL surface are covered, but these visible controller routes are not all driven.

bindings: focus outside editor -> UIDL owns input
unshifted Tab in editor -> insert tab-stop spaces
ordinary editor input -> direct textarea fast path
unhandled direct input -> fall through to UIDL
Tab -> insert spaces through the next configured tab stop
unmodified printable input without selection -> textarea plus single-row fast repaint
Enter at the last visible line -> textarea plus two-row fast repaint
all other ordinary editor input -> textarea widget handling

not_applicable.providers: Pad consumes VFS and semantic resource sessions; it has no selectable provider surface.

preserve: Multi-buffer editing, selection, undo/redo, cut/copy/paste and current/open-buffer search
New/open/save/save-as/save-all, exact large fragmented writes and file identity
Go-to line/file, tab navigation, view toggles and build-diagnostic navigation
Canonical/traversal path handling, oversize/short/throw reads and source blocking
Atomic replacement recovery, save failure rollback, exact cleanup and retry
All sixteen newline-dense 64-KiB buffers with selector/free-list cleanup
pad.document.open, pad.document.active and pad.document.text capability identities and resource.open intent
Semantic snapshot/replace, stale dirty refusal, safe reload, post-commit local-advance recovery and export
Daybook-to-Pad route, competing commit non-clobber, ordinary VFS route from FExplorer and Desk relaunch

prerequisites.trigger: Applet-specific Pad close, prompt or controller ownership changes.
prerequisites.characterization: Drive Save All and dirty applet close through cancel, save/discard and final close outcomes.
prerequisites.reason: L9 preserves Pad's callback and controller implementation; current storage and resource-session evidence does not directly drive these visible lifecycle paths.

prerequisites.trigger: A shared menu, prompt, textarea or tab facility changes one of these dispatch paths.
prerequisites.characterization: Before the relevant touch, drive Close Tab/All, copy/cut/paste, delete line, replace, go-to-file, pane/hidden toggles and next/previous tab.
prerequisites.reason: Their exact UIDL actions and shortcuts are pinned, but current smoke does not execute every route.

bindings: printable ASCII -> replace cell and edit

not_applicable.providers: Grid owns one local CSV-backed sheet and has no selectable provider surface.

preserve: Current A1 formula grammar, dependency results, depth, cycle and error behavior
Strict single-sheet CSV parse/serialize and current editing, navigation and recalculation shortcuts
Exact save/reload, corrupt-source blocking, recovery, rollback and resize behavior
Five current Grid capability IDs, kinds, schemas, effects, flags and handlers
resource.open and resource.reveal source intents remain available

prerequisites.trigger: Any Grid capability or routing code is changed.
prerequisites.characterization: Execute all five handlers: cell.set-selected, cell.selected, workbook.csv, source and workbook.save.
prerequisites.reason: Current Agent hardening pins descriptor exposure, not handler parity.

prerequisites.trigger: Applet-specific Grid close or input dispatch changes.
prerequisites.characterization: Prove dirty close/defer/confirm/discard; add only the touched Tab, Shift-Tab, Page, Home/End, F2 or printable-replace controller cases.
prerequisites.reason: L9 preserves Grid's callback and input implementation; the Grid contract proves clean close and the smoke drives the main path, not every controller edge.

not_applicable.providers: FExplorer operates on its injected VFS and publishes resource capabilities; it has no selectable provider surface.

preserve: Tree/details/preview, sort variants, hidden toggle, goto, properties and parent navigation
Create, rename, delete, copy, cut and paste with exact UI menus/keys
Standalone Go To/preview/create/rename/copy/paste/delete and Desk pointer/focus plus FExplorer-to-Pad route
Chunked read-back verified copy and cut delete ordering
Guard, context, FD, CWD and VFS-selector cleanup
Progress, sync, corruption, throw and cleanup-after-effect failures preserve committed/residue facts exactly
Reveal, selected-resource and bounded UTF-8 preview capability identities
Null selection, malformed bytes, overlong path and read-throw exact-once cleanup behavior
Selected resources route through Desk to Pad

prerequisites.trigger: Scoped VFS conversion touches create, rename or delete rather than the already-gated transfer/preview paths.
prerequisites.characterization: Inject failures into the touched create/rename/delete operation and pin its visible status plus cleanup/residue facts.
prerequisites.reason: Transfer faults are strong; these smaller mutation fault edges are not equivalent evidence.

prerequisites.characterization: Dispatch fexplorer.resource.reveal for valid, absent and invalid resources and prove selection/view results.
prerequisites.reason: The descriptor is exposed but its handler is not currently executed.

prerequisites.trigger: Shared menu/list/tab/controller extraction touches these actions.
prerequisites.characterization: Drive the touched sort, Properties, Backspace, hidden-toggle, cut and menu route before changing it.
prerequisites.reason: The exact UIDL surface is pinned, while modern end-to-end coverage is partial.

not_applicable.providers: Desk composes and retains provider sources for child applets, but it is a host/product composition rather than a selectable domain provider.

preserve: Alt+1..9 focus, Alt+Tab cycling, Alt+M minimize, Alt+R restore, Alt+F full frame, Alt+L layout, Alt+W close, Alt+H launcher and Alt+A/Ctrl+Space Agent composer
Dynamic vertical/horizontal tiling, focus, minimize/restore, full-frame, taskbar pointer activation and focus-before-child pointer delivery
Terminal resize preserves child state and leaves one exact live taskbar projection
Durable catalog, hotbar and nonblocking launcher; enabled/pinned/autostart/quarantine policy
Transactional child launch, close/relaunch, component/resource cleanup and borrowed UIDL ownership
Build and Install resolves and launches a local applet without evaluating catalog text
Exact eleven Desk-private service IDs, exact lookup, dynamic availability, duplicate/full statuses and teardown wipe
Agent provider/runtime/tool/access policy, Daybook resource offer, request bus, XIO and frozen trust composition
Practice recovery/fallback, Daybook/Pad shared resource, Agent hardening and Streams close/relaunch journeys

not_applicable.providers: SoundLab composes neutral audio engines and the machine AudioOut service; it has no selectable provider surface.

applets.selection_policy: Do not select SoundLab as an L2, L3 or L5 proving consumer unless the matching prerequisite is characterized first.

preserve: Shape/frequency/amplitude/duration controls, stale indication and transactional render/analysis
Waveform, landmarks, frequency/centroid metrics, exact WAV save and reveal
AudioOut submit status, generation, rate/channels/frames, nonzero PCM and responsive resize
Ten current soundlab.* capability IDs and the applet component/entry surface
Failed work must not replace the last successful render
Atomic WAV output, unsaved-close negotiation and stop-only-owned AudioOut generation remain required semantics

prerequisites.trigger: SoundLab is selected as a checked-writer or scoped-VFS consumer in L2/L3.
prerequisites.characterization: Prove failed render retains prior PCM; inject replacement interruption, ambiguity, save failure and output-blocked states with exact status/recovery.
prerequisites.reason: The current smoke is a strong happy path, not output failure coverage.

prerequisites.trigger: SoundLab is selected for interop builder conversion.
prerequisites.characterization: Pin and execute all ten capability descriptors, schemas, effects, flags and status translations.
prerequisites.reason: Capability IDs are source-pinned but none has execution parity.

prerequisites.trigger: Applet-specific SoundLab close, lifecycle or AudioOut ownership changes.
prerequisites.characterization: Drive unsaved close and shutdown; prove stop affects only SoundLab's owned generation, including after-effect host starts and absent/device-failure states.
prerequisites.reason: L9 deliberately leaves SoundLab for the later MediaLab rebuild and preserves its opaque callback boundary; current smoke does not exercise close or ownership failure edges.
